panels.description: Client connections per second by connection disposition for the selected service. Calculated from a linear rate estimation based on a cumulative total of each connection disposition.
panels.title: Connections per Second

panels.description: Requests per second for the selected service by HTTP response code class. Calculated from a linear rate estimation based on a cumulative count of each response type.
panels.title: Requests per Second

panels.description: Traffic rates for the selected service as observed for client connections. Calculated from a linear rate estimation based on a cumulative total of bytes for each traffic direction.
panels.title: Bytes per Second

panels.description: The total number of healthy and unhealthy health checks for the service as observed at the Tier 2 layer. The total number will most often be the number of Tier 2 nodes multiplied by the number of Backend Pools. For example, a service with 10 backend pools running on a cluster with 3 Tier 2 nodes will have a total of 30 health checks that should be healthy at any given time.
panels.title: Health Status

panels.description: The HTTP request latency over time for the selected service. Uses a dynamic moving average of request counts that fall within preconfigured histogram buckets in order to compute estimated quantiles (p90, p95, p99 etc.) The latency values seen here may be an overestimate or underestimate depending upon the relative count of requests that fell within each bucket during a given time interval.
panels.title: Round Trip Service Latency

panels.description: The estimated latency within the ILB layer 7 datapath for the selected service. Computed by taking the average duration required from request time until a response has been sent, and subtracting the average duration from the time that the request is forwarded to the backend until a response is recieved from the backend.
panels.title: Load Balancer Datapath Latency

panels.description: The upstream request latency over time for the selected service. For each time interval, the number of requests that fell within a given latency bucket are shown, and stacked vertically. There will usually be a "bright line" across the visualization which indicates the typical latency for the service. When there are resource constraints or other issues with the backend service, the bright areas will shift towards higher latencies.
panels.title: Round Trip Latency Heatmap

panels.description: The rate of 4xx and 5xx errors per second for the selected service. Calculated from a linear rate estimation based on a cumulative total of each error type.
panels.title: Errors Per Second

panels.description: The rate of various HTTP error conditions will appear here for the selected service. Examples include connection reset, protocol errors, and request body too large. These are calculated from a linear rate estimation based on a cumulative total of each.
panels.title: Errors

panels.description: The number of connections per second for each HTTP protocol version for the selected service (HTTP/1.x, HTTP/2, HTTP/3). Calculated from a linear rate estimation based on a cumulative total of each.
panels.title: Client Protocol Version

panels.description: The rate of TLS handshakes for the selected service. Calculated from a linear rate estimation based on a cumulative total.
panels.title: Handshake

panels.description: Cumulative counts of each unique cipher suite negotiated with clients for the service.
panels.title: Ciphers

panels.description: The rate of various TLS error conditions will appear here for the selected service. Examples include connection error, no certificate, certificate verify error, and SAN verification error. These are calculated from a linear rate estimation based on a cumulative total of each.
panels.title: TLS errors

panels.description: An estimate of the TCP throughput for the selected TCP service which is using Tier 2 of the Isovalent Load Balancer. This metric will only appear for such "TCP on t2" services. Uses a linear rate estimation based on a cumulative count of bytes for each direction of traffic.
panels.title: Throughput

panels.description: The count of active client TCP connections for the selected service over time. Computed by subtracting the total number of closed connections from the total number of connections observed for the service at each point in time.
panels.title: Active Connections

panels.description: The number of opened and closed connections per second for the selected service. Calculated from a linear rate estimation based on a cumulative total of each.
panels.title: Connections per Second

panels.description: The total number of healthy and unhealthy health checks for the service as observed at the Tier 2 layer. The total number will most often be the number of Tier 2 nodes multiplied by the number of Backend Pools. For example, a service with 10 backend pools running on a cluster with 3 Tier 2 nodes will have a total of 30 health checks that should be healthy at any given time.
panels.title: Health Status

panels.description: Estimated CPU usage per node for the entire Load Balancer. Calculated as the inverse of idle CPU time based on a linear estimation of total idle CPU time.
panels.title: CPU Usage %

panels.description: An estimate of memory usage percentage for each node in the Load Balancer cluster. Calculated as total memory minus the current value of (free + buffers + cached) memory, and converted to a percentage.
panels.title: Memory Usage %

panels.description: An estimate of disk usage percentage over time. Calculated by subtracting the count of free bytes on a selected mountpoint on each node (summed over all devices with that mountpoint) from the total size of the devices with that mountpoint. The hidden variable in the settings for this dashboard, "mountpoint", must be set to a valid value, in order for this metric to show data.

targets.expr:
((node_filesystem_size_bytes{mountpoint="${mountpoint}"} - node_filesystem_free_bytes{mountpoint="${mountpoint}"}) 
 / node_filesystem_size_bytes{mountpoint="${mountpoint}"} * 100)
* on (instance) group_left(node, device) (
  label_replace(
    max by (address, node) (kube_node_status_addresses{node=~".*", type="InternalIP"}),
    "instance", "$1:9100", "address", "(.*)"
  )
) or on() clamp_max(absent(node_filesystem_size_bytes{node="offline"}),0)

panels.title: Disk Usage %

panels.description: An estimate of the current disk I/O latency for each node in the Isovalent Load Balancer cluster. Calculated from a linear rate estimation based on a cumulative total of disk wait time. The maximum disk latency at each time step over all devices is shown.
panels.title: Disk Wait Time

panels.description: An estimate of the total disk write rate for all devices for each node in the Isovalent Load Balancer cluster. Calculated from a linear rate estimation based on a cumulative total of bytes written to each node.

targets.expr:
sum by (node) ((rate(node_disk_written_bytes_total[$__rate_interval]))
* on (instance) group_left(node) (
  label_replace(
    max by (address, node) (kube_node_status_addresses{node=~".*", type="InternalIP"}),
    "instance", "$1:9100", "address", "(.*)"
  )
)) or on() clamp_max(absent(node_filesystem_size_bytes{node="offline"}),0)

panels.title: Disk Write Rate

panels.description: An estimate of the total disk read rate for all devices for each node in the Isovalent Load Balancer cluster. Calculated from a linear rate estimation based on a cumulative total of bytes read from each node.

targets.expr:
sum by (node) ((rate(node_disk_read_bytes_total[$__rate_interval]))
* on (instance) group_left(node) (
  label_replace(
    max by (address, node) (kube_node_status_addresses{node=~".*", type="InternalIP"}),
    "instance", "$1:9100", "address", "(.*)"
  )
)) or on() clamp_max(absent(node_filesystem_size_bytes{node="offline"}),0)

panels.title: Disk Read Rate

panels.description: The current count of active nodes. Calculated from the running state of the primary agent on each node.
panels.title: Active Nodes

panels.description: The current count of Up and Down BGP session for each node.
panels.title: BGP Sessions by Node

panels.description: The current count of advertised BGP routes for each node.
panels.title: BGP Advertised Routes by Node

panels.description: Total Tier 1 traffic across the entire Isovalent Load Balancer cluster. Calculated from a linear rate estimation based on a cumulative total of each traffic direction.
panels.title: Tier 1 Traffic

panels.description: An estimate of the total traffic rate per network interface per node. Calculated from a linear rate estimation based on a cumulative total.
panels.title: Total Bytes by Interface

panels.description: An estimate of the receive rate per network interface per node. Calculated from a linear rate estimation based on a cumulative total.
panels.title: Receive Bytes by Interface

panels.description: An estimate of the transmit rate per network interface per node. Calculated from a linear rate estimation based on a cumulative total.
panels.title: Transmit Bytes by Interface

panels.description: An estimate of the total packet rate per network interface per node. Calculated from a linear rate estimation based on a cumulative total.
panels.title: Total Packets by Interface

panels.description: An estimate of the receive pakcet rate per network interface per node. Calculated from a linear rate estimation based on a cumulative total.
panels.title: Receive Packets by Interface

panels.description: An estimate of the transmit packet rate per network interface per node. Calculated from a linear rate estimation based on a cumulative total.
panels.title: Transmit Packets by Interface

list.description: Namespace which contains Isovalent Load Balancer services
list.label: Namespace
list.name: namespace

list.description: The warning threshold latency displayed in the latency panels (in milliseconds)
list.label: Latency Threshold (ms)
list.name: latency_threshold

list.description: The name of the Isovalent Load Balancer service.
list.label: Service Name
list.name: cluster_name

list.description: The name of the Isovalent Load Balancer service connection manager
list.label: Connection Manager Name
list.name: connection_manager_prefix

list.description: Enter a valid mount point of a filesystem that represents disk usage %. For example "/" or "/var"
list.label: Mount Point for Disk Usage %
list.name: mountpoint
